prompt: We have a Django monolith handling our e-commerce platform. It's getting slow under load (~500 req/s) and the team has grown to 12 devs stepping on each other. Should we move to microservices?

assertions.name: has_constraint_analysis
assertions.description: Explicitly analyzes constraints (team size, current scale, pain points) before recommending

assertions.name: has_architectural_style_recommendation
assertions.description: Recommends a specific approach with reasoning tied to the dual problems (perf + team coordination)

assertions.name: has_alternatives_considered
assertions.description: Discusses alternatives to microservices (caching, read replicas, modular monolith) and when they'd suffice

assertions.name: has_layer_structure
assertions.description: Describes how the evolved architecture layers connect

assertions.name: has_cross_cutting_concerns
assertions.description: Addresses at least 2 of: auth, error handling, caching, config

assertions.name: has_actionable_next_steps
assertions.description: Provides concrete migration steps or handoff guidance, not just abstract theory
